compiled_state.title: As a developer planning an epic, brainstorm verifies external tool feasibility before the spec is finalized
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Create dso:feasibility-reviewer dedicated plugin agent and integrate as 4th brainstorm reviewer.
**Why**: Catches unverified integration assumptions before they become specs — the core failure that caused the v2-v3 migration disaster.
**Scope**:
- IN: Agent definition file, brainstorm SKILL.md modification, keyword trigger + feasibility_review_recommended trigger, WebSearch for tool verification and GitHub code search, Technical Feasibility section output, high-risk flagging with spike recommendation
- OUT: Preplanning research (separate story), complexity evaluator changes (S1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, feasibility reviewer is dispatched in brainstorm Phase 2 Step 3 (Fidelity Review) as a 4th parallel reviewer, using the same Task tool dispatch pattern as the existing 3 reviewers ← Satisfies SC1
- When this story is complete, the reviewer uses WebSearch to verify tool capabilities and find known-working code ← Satisfies SC1
- When this story is complete, the reviewer produces a Technical Feasibility section with verified constraints ← Satisfies SC1
- When this story is complete, critical capability gaps flag the epic as high-risk with spike recommendation ← Satisfies SC1
- When this story is complete, epics without integration signals skip the reviewer ← Satisfies SC1
- When feasibility_review_recommended is absent from evaluator output, brainstorm falls back to keyword-only trigger for feasibility reviewer dispatch ← backward compatibility
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] WebSearch-dependent behavior — tests must validate agent output without live network calls; use deterministic fixtures
- [Reliability] WebSearch may return no results — agent needs graceful fallback (flag as high-risk, do not block)

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks completed and committed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
